priorities: Securing and accelerating research for Chloe's early‑onset Parkinson’s disease
Maintaining large‑cap pharmaceutical holdings with strong neuroscience pipelines
Ensuring long‑term, data‑driven stability of the family’s wealth and wealth‑transfer plan
Preserving operational precision and steady growth in the fixed‑income sleeve
redLines: Divest from any company that abandons or reduces funding for Parkinson’s or broader neuro‑degenerative research
Reduce exposure to large‑cap pharma stocks that do not have a clear neuroscience R&D commitment
Shift capital away from “cheap lifestyle” or consumer‑discretionary businesses that prioritize short‑term profits over research
preferredSectors: Large‑cap pharmaceuticals / healthcare with neuroscience focus
Biotech firms dedicated to neuro‑degenerative disease research
Stable, capital‑intensive industrial assets (automotive supply) for diversification
avoidedSectors: Consumer‑discretionary / lifestyle companies lacking research spend
High‑volatility speculative biotech not aligned with neuro‑research
esgFocus: Health – specifically neuro‑degenerative disease research and treatment

investmentBehavior.timeHorizon: Long‑term (10+ years) focused on multigenerational wealth preservation and a cure for Parkinson’s
investmentBehavior.liquidity: Moderate – sufficient cash flow for regular grant funding and family needs while keeping the bulk in stable equity/bond sleeves

lifeEvents.description: Daughter Chloe was studying abroad and doing well.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: No immediate change; reinforces family‑centric outlook.

lifeEvents.description: In‑depth discussion on long‑term wealth transmission for the children, with a 12% allocation to global healthcare giants.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Solidifies healthcare exposure as a core pillar for future generations.

lifeEvents.description: Chloe diagnosed with rapidly progressing early‑onset Parkinson’s disease.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Triggers a strategic pivot toward neuro‑research investments and philanthropy.

lifeEvents.description: Creation of a Venture Philanthropy foundation dedicated to neuro‑degenerative research; explicit mandate to align core holdings with this cause.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Reallocation of capital toward pharma/biotech firms with strong neurology pipelines; heightened monitoring of R&D commitments.

lifeEvents.description: CHF 1 million transferred from private portfolio to the family foundation as an immediate grant to a neuro‑genetics lab in Geneva.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Demonstrates willingness to move liquid assets quickly for targeted research funding.

lifeEvents.description: Attendance at a medical charity gala; reiterated need for capital to flow to companies leading the fight against Parkinson’s.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Reinforces ongoing commitment to keep large‑cap pharma exposure aligned with neuro‑research.

lifeEvents.description: Meeting with a biotech firm developing a breakthrough Parkinson’s asset; expressed desire for large‑cap holdings to act as primary financial support.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Potential future co‑investment or strategic partnership with breakthrough neuro‑biotech.

personalProfile.interests: automotive industry, neuroscience research, art sponsorship
personalProfile.quirks: becomes intensely emotional when family health is involved; frequently checks corporate credit card limits for family travel
personalProfile.rapportTriggers: mention of neuro‑degenerative research, large‑cap pharma R&D, family wellbeing, data‑driven performance reports
personalProfile.avoidTopics: competitor banks, sports rivalries, non‑research‑focused consumer brands

keyQuotes: Our private wealth is now a weapon to save Chloe.
I want our core healthcare and pharma holdings to actively support the entities pushing the boundaries of brain disease research.
If a company we own ever abandons or defunds Parkinson's research to chase cheap lifestyle profits, it would feel like a personal betrayal of my daughter.
We must keep our capital flowing to the companies leading the fight against this disease.
